GET /v1/trust-frameworks.description: List the global reference trust frameworks Didit ID recognizes (eIDAS 2.0 / EUDI, ISO/IEC 18013-5 mDL, Swiss e-ID, US mDL / AAMVA). These are read-only reference rows seeded on first access — they are not tenant-scoped — and are used to tag a trusted issuer with the framework it operates under. The response is a bare JSON array of framework objects ordered by name.

200.description: A bare JSON array of trust framework objects, ordered alphabetically by name.

items.description: Array of trust framework reference objects.

id.description: Stable UUID of the trust framework row.

slug.description: URL-safe unique identifier for the framework; use this value as the `framework` field when registering a trusted issuer.

region.description: Geographic region or jurisdiction the framework applies to (e.g. EU, CH, US, Global).

profile.description: Interoperability profile the framework follows (e.g. HAIP, ISO mDL, AAMVA, national).

formats.description: Credential formats supported under this framework (e.g. sd_jwt_vc, mdoc).

description.description: Short prose description of the framework.

GET /v1/trusted-issuers.description: List the issuers this tenant trusts when verifying presented credentials. During verification, Didit ID rejects any credential whose `iss` is not an active trusted issuer (the supply-chain-of-trust gate). The tenant's own self-trust row is guaranteed to exist so the registry is never empty. Scoped to the caller's tenant via the API key. Returns a bare JSON array ordered by most-recently-created first.

200.description: A bare JSON array of trusted issuer objects for the caller's tenant, newest first.

id.description: UUID of the trusted issuer registry entry.

name.description: Display name of the trusted issuer.

iss.description: The HTTPS `iss` identifier of the issuer; credentials are matched against this value during verification.

trust_anchor.description: How the issuer's signing key is anchored and resolved. `https-jwks` resolves keys from `jwks_uri`; `did-web` resolves from the issuer DID; `x509` uses a certificate chain.

jwks_uri.description: JWKS endpoint used to fetch the issuer's public keys (populated when `trust_anchor` is `https-jwks`). Empty string when not set.

did.description: Decentralized identifier of the issuer (populated when `trust_anchor` is `did-web`). Empty string when not set.

status.description: Whether this issuer is currently trusted. Only `active` issuers pass the verification trust gate.

added_by.description: Identifier of the API user / tenant principal that registered this trusted issuer. Empty string for seeded self-trust rows.

framework.description: Slug of the trust framework this issuer operates under, or null if none is associated.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the trusted issuer was registered.

POST /v1/trusted-issuers.description: Register an issuer this tenant will trust when verifying credentials. After registration, credentials whose `iss` matches an active trusted issuer pass the verification trust gate. The new entry is always created with status `active` and `added_by` set to the calling API principal. Scoped to the caller's tenant via the API key.

name.description: Display name for the trusted issuer.

iss.description: The HTTPS `iss` identifier of the issuer to trust. Must be unique within the tenant.

trust_anchor.description: How the issuer's signing key is anchored. Defaults to `https-jwks` when omitted.

jwks_uri.description: JWKS endpoint used to fetch the issuer's public keys. Provide when `trust_anchor` is `https-jwks`.

did.description: Decentralized identifier of the issuer. Provide when `trust_anchor` is `did-web`.

framework.description: Trust framework slug (from GET /v1/trust-frameworks) to associate with this issuer (e.g. eidas2). Resolved to a framework if it exists; ignored if no match is found.

201.description: The trusted issuer was registered. Returns the full trusted issuer object.

id.description: UUID of the newly created trusted issuer registry entry.

name.description: Display name of the trusted issuer.

iss.description: The HTTPS `iss` identifier of the issuer.

trust_anchor.description: How the issuer's signing key is anchored and resolved.

jwks_uri.description: JWKS endpoint for the issuer's public keys; empty string when not set.

did.description: Issuer DID; empty string when not set.

status.description: Trust status of the issuer. Newly created issuers are always `active`.

added_by.description: Identifier of the API principal that registered this trusted issuer.

framework.description: Slug of the associated trust framework, or null if none.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the trusted issuer was registered.

DELETE /v1/trusted-issuers/{uuid}.description: Remove an issuer from this tenant's trusted-issuer registry (soft delete). After removal, credentials from this issuer no longer pass the verification trust gate. Scoped to the caller's tenant — a UUID belonging to a different tenant returns 404. Returns 204 No Content on success with an empty body.

params.name: uuid
params.description: UUID of the trusted issuer registry entry to remove.

204.description: The trusted issuer was removed (soft-deleted). No response body is returned.

POST /v1/trusted-issuers/{uuid}/validate.description: Re-check that a registered trusted issuer is reachable and its trust anchor resolves. Returns the full trusted issuer object plus a `validation` block summarizing the check. Scoped to the caller's tenant — a UUID belonging to a different tenant returns 404.

params.name: uuid
params.description: UUID of the trusted issuer to validate.

200.description: The trusted issuer object with an appended `validation` block describing the re-check result.

id.description: UUID of the trusted issuer registry entry.

name.description: Display name of the trusted issuer.

iss.description: The HTTPS `iss` identifier of the issuer.

trust_anchor.description: How the issuer's signing key is anchored and resolved.

jwks_uri.description: JWKS endpoint for the issuer's public keys; empty string when not set.

did.description: Issuer DID; empty string when not set.

added_by.description: Identifier of the API principal that registered this trusted issuer.

framework.description: Slug of the associated trust framework, or null if none.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the trusted issuer was registered.

validation.description: Summary of the freshly run trust-anchor re-check.

reachable.description: Whether the issuer endpoint (e.g. its JWKS or DID document) was reachable.

anchor_ok.description: Whether the issuer's trust anchor resolved and validated successfully.

status.description: The current registry status of the issuer at validation time.

GET /v1/relying-parties.description: List the relying parties (verifiers) registered under this tenant. A relying party's web origin is checked when it requests a credential presentation over the Digital Credentials API. Scoped to the caller's tenant via the API key. Returns a bare JSON array ordered by most-recently-created first.

200.description: A bare JSON array of relying party objects for the caller's tenant, newest first.

id.description: UUID of the relying party registration.

name.description: Display name of the relying party / verifier.

origin.description: The registered web origin of the relying party, checked on presentation requests.

client_id.description: OAuth/OpenID4VP client identifier for the relying party. Empty string when not set.

status.description: Whether the relying party registration is currently active.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the relying party was registered.

POST /v1/relying-parties.description: Register a relying party (verifier) under this tenant. Its web origin is validated when it requests a credential presentation over the Digital Credentials API. The new registration is always created with status `active`. Scoped to the caller's tenant via the API key.

name.description: Display name for the relying party / verifier.

origin.description: Web origin of the relying party; this is the value checked on DC-API presentation requests.

client_id.description: OAuth/OpenID4VP client identifier for the relying party.

201.description: The relying party was registered. Returns the full relying party object.

id.description: UUID of the newly created relying party registration.

name.description: Display name of the relying party / verifier.

origin.description: The registered web origin of the relying party.

client_id.description: OAuth/OpenID4VP client identifier for the relying party; empty string when not set.

status.description: Status of the relying party registration. Newly created relying parties are always `active`.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the relying party was registered.

DELETE /v1/relying-parties/{uuid}.description: Remove a relying party registration from this tenant (soft delete). After removal, the verifier's origin is no longer recognized on presentation requests. Scoped to the caller's tenant — a UUID belonging to a different tenant returns 404. Returns 204 No Content on success with an empty body.

params.name: uuid
params.description: UUID of the relying party registration to remove.

204.description: The relying party registration was removed (soft-deleted). No response body is returned.
